summary: Regression pass after adding error logging to previously-empty catch blocks. All backend endpoints (GET /api/demo-songs, GET /api/songs, GET/PUT /api/settings) return 200 with expected data. Full frontend flow verified: NEON.KEYS header, 5 demo songs, 88-key piano, song selection, play/pause/resume/stop, currentTime resets to 0:00, speed slider updates (1.55x), settings modal (volume slider, sustain switch, color option) all functional, piano-key-C4 click triggers note. Zero console.error and zero app-originated console.warn observed. Only 2 warnings present are benign browser-level Chrome AudioContext autoplay-policy warnings (not from the newly added logging).
passed: GET /api/demo-songs -> 200, 5 songs including Twinkle Twinkle Little Star
Home page loads with NEON.KEYS header and piano rendered
Twinkle Twinkle Little Star selectable from library
play-button starts playback (notes falling, key highlighted)
settings-button opens settings; volume-slider, sustain-switch, color-option all interact
piano-key-C4 click triggers note without console error
0 console errors during full flow; new console.warn catch-block additions did NOT fire under normal operation
notes: The 2 observed console warnings are Chrome AudioContext autoplay-policy notices originating from the browser itself (pre-existing behavior, not from the new console.warn statements in usePianoEngine.js/PianoApp.jsx). No regressions detected from the code quality changes.
